reasoning: Candidate (right) is a warm sandy/beige/pink wall texture that fails on nearly every style axis vs the peer set (left). (1) Palette overlap: near-zero — peers are cool gray-dominant with teal/cyan accents and high contrast darks; candidate is warm sandy beige with pink tones, a completely different hue family. (2) Pixel density: candidate has sparse, uniform block pattern with very few sub-tile features; peers are densely detailed at the 32x32 scale. (3) Shading technique: peers use hard-stepped pixel art contrast with clear dark-to-light edges; candidate looks soft and low-contrast, lacking distinct pixel art stepping. (4) Warmth/saturation bias: peers are cool and desaturated (dungeon stone); candidate is warm and relatively saturated — opposite direction. (5) Hand-pixeled charm: candidate reads as procedural or texture-fill, missing the deliberate pixel-by-pixel character of peers. (6) Photo-realism contamination: low but the uniform block tiling lacks intentional pixel craft. (7) Overall feel: candidate looks like a sandy outdoor wall from a bright desert game, not the dark dungeon/ruin aesthetic of the 'Echoes outdoor' peer family. This is a fundamental style mismatch, not a surface-type difference.
